  • Constructor Details

    • soulListener

      public soulListener()

  • Method Details

    • registerSouls

      public static void registerSouls()
      Registers all predefined soul-type instances to the SOULS map. Each soul type defined in the SoulTypes enumeration is associated with a corresponding instance of its specific implementation. This method is static and initializes the mapping of soul types to their respective implementations, such as Red, Green, and Blue, among others. The mapping includes both base variants (e.g., BaseRed, BaseGreen) and main variants of soul types. Once this method has been invoked, the SOULS map will contain all predefined relationship bindings between SoulTypes and their respective concrete implementations.

    • getSoul

      public static Base_Soul getSoul(org.bukkit.entity.Player player)
      Retrieves the soul associated with the given player based on their persistent data. This method attempts to find the player's soul by querying the PersistentDataContainer of the provided Player object for a stored soul identifier. If a valid soul identifier is found, it is used to determine the corresponding soul instance from the predefined SOULS map. If no identifier is found or if it cannot be resolved to a valid soul, the method returns null.
      Parameters:
      player - the player whose soul is to be retrieved; must not be null
      Returns:
      the Base_Soul instance associated with the player, or null if no valid soul is found for the player

    • Passive

      public void Passive(org.bukkit.entity.Player player)
      Handles the passive abilities for a player if they are alive and have an associated soul. This method checks whether the provided player is alive using Life_and_Death.is_alive. If the player is alive, their associated soul is obtained via getSoul(Player). If a valid soul instance is found, the Passive method of the soul is invoked.
      Parameters:
      player - the player for whom the passive ability is executed; must not be null

    • onPlayerJoin

      public void onPlayerJoin(org.bukkit.event.player.PlayerJoinEvent event)
      Initial Player Join Function, it sends ResourcePack, then sets default values such as ComorAction, Soulpoints, and Ability One Cooldowns
      Parameters:
      event - Actual Player Join Event, this is called whenever a player Joins

    • onResourcePackResponse

      public void onResourcePackResponse(org.bukkit.event.player.PlayerResourcePackStatusEvent event)

      Parameters:
      event - Event that is called during the ResourcePack Status update

    • onPlayerDamage

      public void onPlayerDamage(org.bukkit.event.entity.EntityDamageEvent event)
      Player Damage SoulPoints: Whenever a player is damaged their soul points go up
      Parameters:
      event - Event that is called during the Entity Damage Event Update

    • onPlayerDamagedEntity

      public void onPlayerDamagedEntity(org.bukkit.event.entity.EntityDamageByEntityEvent event)
      Entity Damage Player Soulpoints: Whenever a player damages an Entity their SoulPoints go up
      Parameters:
      event - Event that is called during the Entity Damaged by Entity Event Update

    • onPlayerMoveEvent

      public void onPlayerMoveEvent(org.bukkit.event.player.PlayerMoveEvent event)
      Player Move Update: Whenever a player moves, this event checks if they have a soul and sets their walk and fly speed accordingly. If they don't have a soul, it opens the starting GUI after a small delay to ensure the resource pack is sent first. It also initializes the ComorAction persistent data if it doesn't exist.
      Parameters:
      event - Event that is called during the Player Move Event Update

    • showSoulPoints

      public static void showSoulPoints(org.bukkit.entity.Player player)
      Shows the SoulPoints of a Given Player Within their UI, and resets it if they do not have it
      Parameters:
      player - to show SoulPoints of

    • enableActivePassives

      public void enableActivePassives(io.papermc.paper.event.player.PlayerArmSwingEvent event)
      Enable the Active Passives of a Player, this is the Event Key for the Player Arm Swing Event Whenever a player swings their arm, this event checks if they have a soul and if they do, it calls the ActivePassive method of the soul. If they don't have a soul, it does nothing. Also it checks ComorAction to see if the method should rlly be run
      Parameters:
      event - Event that is called during the Player Arm Swing Event

    • AbilityOne

      public void AbilityOne(org.bukkit.entity.Player player)
      Ability One: This method is responsible for handling the Ability One functionality of a player. This is the Keyboard / Command Triggered Function
      Parameters:
      player - Player to handle Ability One for

    • AbilityTwo

      public void AbilityTwo(org.bukkit.entity.Player player) throws net.skinsrestorer.api.exception.MineSkinException, net.skinsrestorer.api.exception.DataRequestException
      Ability Two: This method is responsible for handling the Ability Two functionality of a player. This is the Keyboard / Command Triggered Function
      Parameters:
      player - Player to handle Ability Two for
      Throws:
      net.skinsrestorer.api.exception.MineSkinException - Data Exception
      net.skinsrestorer.api.exception.DataRequestException - Data Exception

    • ActivePassive

      public void ActivePassive(org.bukkit.entity.Player player)
      Active Passive: This method is responsible for handling the Active Passive functionality of a player. This is the Keyboard / Command Triggered Function
      Parameters:
      player - the player triggering the active or passive ability; must not be null

    • AbilityOneListener

      public void AbilityOneListener(com.destroystokyo.paper.event.player.PlayerJumpEvent event)
      Ability One Listener, event that is triggered every time a player jumps, checks if they have com or action
      Parameters:
      event - Event that is called during the Player Jump Event Update

    • AbilityTwoListener

      public void AbilityTwoListener(org.bukkit.event.player.PlayerDropItemEvent event) throws net.skinsrestorer.api.exception.MineSkinException, net.skinsrestorer.api.exception.DataRequestException
      Handles the Ability Two action triggered by a player dropping an item. This listener is invoked when a PlayerDropItemEvent is fired. It checks multiple conditions, including the player's state, the soul's state, and specific ability logic, to determine the appropriate actions. The method may cancel the event based on condition evaluations.
      Parameters:
      event - The PlayerDropItemEvent that represents the action of a player dropping an item. This event contains information about the player and the dropped item, which is used for evaluating the ability logic.
      Throws:
      net.skinsrestorer.api.exception.MineSkinException - If there is an issue related to MineSkin operations during ability execution.
      net.skinsrestorer.api.exception.DataRequestException - If there is an issue retrieving required data for the ability.

    • activateAttackPassive

      public void activateAttackPassive(io.papermc.paper.event.player.PrePlayerAttackEntityEvent event)
      Handles the activation of attack passives for a player during a pre-attack event. This method checks the player's state, applies any associated moveset attack passives, and triggers the passive effect of the player's soul if applicable.
      Parameters:
      event - the PrePlayerAttackEntityEvent triggered when a player is about to attack an entity
